pkg/framework/transport.purpose: Namespace-only organisational folder owning the HTTP transport layer for all domain httpdriver and api/v3/handlers packages. Its sole child (httptransport) provides the single generic Handler[Request,Response] decode→operation→encode pipeline every v1 and v3 endpoint in the monorepo is built on — no source files live here directly.

patterns.name: Child-delegated architecture
patterns.description: All source code lives in pkg/framework/transport/httptransport; this folder is a namespace wrapper only. New transport sub-packages (e.g. grpctransport) would sit here as siblings rather than as loose files.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Handler[Request,Response] value type and NewHandler triple constructor (RequestDecoder, Operation, ResponseEncoder); contains the last-resort 500 fallback ErrorEncoder that must not be replicated elsewhere.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not instantiate the handler{} struct directly — always use NewHandler; it attaches defaultHandlerOptions including GenericErrorEncoder.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerWithArgs variant for path-param injection; value type cloned by With()/Chain().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add mutable state (mutex, cache) to HandlerWithArgs — value semantics mean each With()/Chain() call produces a shallow copy that would race on shared pointer fields.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerOption and AppendOptions helpers; controls ErrorEncoder chain construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The ErrorEncoder chain is first-match-wins; option ordering determines which encoder handles an error.

key_file_guides.role: ResponseEncoder and ErrorEncoder function type definitions shared across all handler packages; the separate sub-package breaks the import cycle with commonhttp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The SelfEncodingError escape hatch is for infrastructure types only — do not implement it on domain errors; those belong in the ErrorEncoder chain.

anti_patterns: Placing source .go files directly in pkg/framework/transport/ — all code belongs in named sub-packages.
Adding a second child package that duplicates the decode/operate/encode pipeline instead of reusing httptransport.Handler.
Importing pkg/framework/transport directly (no Go files here) — always import the named sub-package.
Calling context.Background() inside a RequestDecoder — always use the ctx passed from ServeHTTP.
Writing to http.ResponseWriter inside a RequestDecoder on error — return the error and let the ErrorEncoder chain handle it.

decisions.decision: Single child package rather than a flat file set.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the transport abstraction extensible (future grpc/websocket siblings) while letting all current consumers import a stable sub-package path (httptransport) without churn.
Prevents accidental shared state across middleware chains; each Chain() call returns a new handler copy, making the pipeline composable without races.

decisions.decision: Last-resort 500 fallback lives in handler.go, not in GenericErrorEncoder.
decisions.rationale: Keeps GenericErrorEncoder focused on typed domain-error matching; the transport layer owns the final safety net so domain packages never handle unrecognised error types themselves.
